repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md says ExactID catalogue is seed-time empty

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML + ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO + ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML: fixed-overlap orthogonal inference pieces, but not a single triple-frontier theorem

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_bjs_efficient_event_study: estimator IF-gap discipline for staggered DiD, not ExactID weak-overlap ATE studentization; eid_lp_svar_nonequiv: primitive non-equivalence warning in LP/SVAR PartialID, not DML/LRO/ADML

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022RR + HongLeungLi2020 + MaSantAnnaSasakiUra2026WeakOverlapDR + Dorn2025WeakOverlapDR: weak-overlap/Riesz/DR comparators, none gives an iff DML-LRO-ADML variance-rule frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lp_svar_nonequiv and eid_bjs_efficient_event_study warn against assumed margins, but neither gives an ExactID two-cell weak-overlap variance-rule witness

published_prior_art.cite: CrumpHotzImbensMitnik2009 + HongLeungLi2020 + ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022RR: motivate overlap/Riesz instability, not a primitive finite-support DML-LRO-ADML separation witness

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Pin the exact published variance-functional formulas for DML, LRO, and ADML before Stage 0 so that S_{m,n} and its adjoint are not merely named by estimator family.
Add a boundary corollary stating the observable diagnostic in finite-support matrix form for arbitrary J cells, with the two-cell witness as the minimal example.
In the literature comparison, explicitly distinguish Dorn's thresholded AIPW t-statistic from the proposal's unthresholded variance-rule equivalence frontier.

recommended_next_step: Proceed to Stage 0 with emphasis on deriving the variance-rule adjoint matrices from the actual published DML, LRO, and ADML standard-error formulas.
